channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /smg/ - stock market genera — 37 replies — 148.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/ - stock market genera

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-29 15:19 UTC

OP

Too infinity and beyond edish

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars:
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates''

Previous:>>62426231

Early replies (first 3)

62426857
> nows

62426858
>we project chips to grow 1000% per quarter every quarter until 2035 atleast

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62426870
>>62426868
Micron

62426871
This one goes out to my 0 DTE Bros, out there catching that fastball

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VD6izVzuIkU

62426872
>>62426851
>you were told exactly what was going to happen today and you ignored it
Stay poor, losers

62426873
memory and memory accessories

62426874
>MSFT red

62426875
>>62426864
Because you're witnessing his prophecy come true.

Korean government move today and this pumping are the last straw.
It all breaks.

62426877
SOXL used piercing strike!
It was super effective!

62426879
I have recovered a lil. Thank you for your attention to this matter
>>62426858
Now that's a blast from the past

62426883
>>62426879
NOT THE AD REVENUE

62426886
Is the RKLB anon here? Thanks for the recc! Very good call.

Also, time to buy treasury bonds now that the Prez can fire a fed chair at whim

62426888
>>62426879
Jaaaaannnyyyy get this nafo freak banned

62426889
Google the dirty DRAM deal and keep those stop losses tight

62426890
it was nice of them to keep WDC decoupled from the rest just to give us a little memory trade indicator light

62426893
Microdick you bloody bitch!

62426895
>>62426875
>Prussian Socialism incoming

62426896
When will Blue Owl rebound?

62426903
>>62426872
If you bought memory you got fucked.

62426908
GLWniggaz, it's our time

62426909
>Just bought a stock after waiting for an hour for pricedrop that never appeared, only for it to instantly dump
Im tired. So very tired

62426911
>>62426893

the exact bottom. you were supposed to buy there sar.

62426912
SOXL

62426913
Jeremy Grantham says that when SPX is going up but the bubble stocks are red it’s over

62426914
Comcast and Charter up 10% today but crickets from you idiots. Do you nigs even have watchlists that aren’t full of garbage penny stocks?

62426915
>>62426895
Not really.

I mean maybe some sort of culture based around the internet given deteritorialization.

We've had 0 reserve requirements since COVID so money was meaningless anyway, but now with Dons moves its basically sovietized anyway and theres no logic to the incentive system.

Retards here with 80IQs are financially outperforming people with skills doing bluecollar work, and there no risk because they always pump, so you cant even make the risk argument.

It'll collapse soon.
I hope not in favor of the commies theyve got blooming in nyc, but itll collapse.

62426916
>>62426857
kinda gay

62426917
GRRR Chads where you at?

62426918
>>62426914
what site do you use for watchlist? i only have the free version of tradingview and it only gives me 12 stocks to watch. any site you can recomennd saar

62426921
>>62426915

they are going to run the weird faggot from CA in the next election. the one that was dancing during the wildfires that burnt down everyone's houses while talking about selling the land

he definitely rapes babies

62426922
>>62426918
Doesn't your trading app have a watchlist function? I've got a bunch of watchlists in thinkorswim

62426923
>>62426896

I think they are sitting on a mountain of shit when credit tightens.

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 104 replies — 104.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-06-29 14:20 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62415492
>>62415228
Are you white?

62416132
>>62395655
I sold between 90-110k
Bought back between 59-64k
Still 1/3 of Tether left to buy further dips

62416334
>>62411213
Yeah. The Aegis partnership makes me even more interested to see how the TBV public testnet progresses.

62416338
>>62415301
but short term

62416355
>>62416132
Nice trades.

62416385
>>62395655
because I make money trading not holding garbage

62416439
>>62416385
Because I believe Bitcoin is the strongest long-term store of value in crypto. Short-term volatility doesn't change why I bought it in the first place.

62416488
>>62411265
I'm not holding bitcoin for next week. I'm holding it for the next decade.

62416497
>>62411213
I have doubts for Aave coin

62416506
Because every major dip has looked scary in the moment, but the long-term trend is what matters to me.

62416528
eistein coin

62417470
>>62416385
Garbage? Seriously?

62417484
>>62416497
Nobody's saying you have to like the coin. The point is the protocol and why it still dominates DeFi lending.

62417496
>>62411241
your choice

62417853
>>62415297
Memecoins are different

62419146
>>62395655
Because I'm not a retard

62419150
I know the maxis wont want to watch this…but btc is fundamentally flawed, as are all pow and pos consensus mechanisms…it’s not gonna work long term
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=fo8ZScrXFZE&ra=m
Jesus loves you, Christ is King

62419226
>>62395655
I dont hold anything with zero value...maybe trade it

62419255
>>62419226
That's funny. From $1 to $100k and zero value?

62419275
>>62419255
Zero value as in speculative/doesnt generate a cashflow...sorry you know nothing about finance.

62420185
>>62419146
Non coiner. Ok

62421786
>>62419150
Hmm, that's your opinion.

62422222
>>62409106
>he doesnt realize 1 mcmansion is not enough and they need the price to go up into infinity
fucking bitcuck

62422228
Holy shit guys. sound the alarm bell. Someone just pointed out that managers in a company want to get paid for their efforts... No wonder why all stocks in the world are in a perpetual downtrend. We've finally figured it out, all thanks to a bitcuck. It was the damn managers ALL ALONG! using the stocks as a money printer. Better fucking buy some BITCOIN to get myself out of this mess. Lord what have I done? This market failure is finally solved by BITCOIN. Oh lord thank you lord. I have seen the light. Thank god for saving me from those evil damn managers who keep dumping their stocks on me. Trillions of liquidity, all harvested by CEOs... LET BANKS FALL! BITCOIN TO DA MOON!

62422277
>>62422228
So you just realized just NOW?

62422765
>>62419275
People only need x1000 so it doesn't matter.

62422776
>>62416506
??

62423752
>>62419226
How come?

62426458
>>62422765
Makes sense lol

62426616
to the moon

item.title: There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women. — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women.

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-29 15:10 UTC

OP

There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women. This is what everyone wants, what literally the entire economy was built to acquire. Every skyscraper you see was designed and erected for the sole purpose of achieving the aforementioned creampie experience. Every other currency is a proxy for the real one. Gold, USD, Bitcoin, none of it has "real" value. Only the bath of pleasure that comes from nutting in a pretty woman does. Orient your investment philosophy around this fact sooner rather than later.

Early replies (first 3)

62425808
>>62425803
Ok saaar, just sold my pregnantbutt for pretty girl puss!

62425825
>>62425803
Dude creampie feel the best when you are the 18yo not post wall.

62425847
>>62425803
you are an r-selected retard who cannot think 30 seconds ahead

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62425866
>>62425803
Circumcision was invented precisely to devalue said currency in favor of the rest.

62426162
>creampies
that's called normal sex you virgin porn addict retard

62426176
>>62425803
“Muh Dick”. Grow up. Women are awful except for when you are age 15-35. Before and after those years they are a huge fucking liability.

62426179
>>62425803
women and their holes stink and are unbearable.

62426187
>>62425803
So are arranged marriages a kind of socialism in this premise?

62426195
Ricky handing out creampies to all the women you want to fuck lol truth hurts.

62426198
> the asshole is less than 1 inch away from the "good parts"

62426228
>>62425808
>>62425825
>>62425847
>>62426162
>>62426176
>>62426179
>>62426187
>>62426195
>>62426198
Your efforts to convince yourselves that you don't want to creampie beautiful women are laughable.

62426237
>>62425803
Correct.

62426240
>>62425803
When i serve my creampies I prefer surface level creampies.

62426243
>>62425803
The reality that christcucks, wagies, and wifeguys will never admit is that having sex with milfs all day is all that matters in life: everything else is cope. That's why the male pornstar or man that goes to prostitutes is more free than they will ever be.

62426263
>>62426243
They are all stuck inside thought-prisons to keep society running. They are literal slaves to the optimization algorithm of civilization, and their chains are just ideas like "I must not sin", "I must contribute", "I'm better than hedonists", etc. They torture themselves into a horrific denial of their own biology. Hunter-gatherers would see them the way we imagine future digital mind uploads being punished by evil AIs.

62426271
i remember when I was like 12 I concluded that the meaning of life was the orgasm.

62426283
>>62425803
Anon, we're not rabbits. Creampies don't matter, raising the kids does. The true currency is having a grandchild, because you know your kid isn't a degenerate failure if that happens

62426290
>>62426271
You were correct then. But society needed you to wage, and that meant torturing you into accepting fake goals instead of creampie-ish ones.

62426295
>>62426283
Pure copium. In your heart of hearts you know this, anon.

62426305
i have dik and I must coom

62426328
>>62426305
You'd better while you are young, or you will sorely regret it.

62426382
https://youtu.be/nql07h-cPF0

62426414
We appreciate posts like this

62426770
>>62426414
Good because there's a lot more of them coming.

62426869
>>62425803
She isn't attractive

62426885
Actually no, this is the mind of a coombrained goyslave and why you will never accomplish anything.

item.title: Stinky — 38 replies — 92.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Stinky

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-06-29 14:55 UTC

OP

Is there any legitimate reason to continue long-term holding Chainlink? Have had my 10k stack since 2018 and if/when another bull market happens, I think I'm ready to unload the position. The DTCC/Swift shit has done nothing for the token so far. I'd like to at least have a down payment for a home after a decade of bag holding...my dreams of generational wealth are gone.

Not meant to be a fud thread btw, I just don't even follow the crypto space anymore after so many years without meaningful enterprise adoption.

Early replies (first 3)

62424635
>>62424625
>Is there any legitimate reason to continue long-term holding Chainlink?
Yes : it never ever goes down below $6. $6 is basically 0, so by buying now you know you are slurping near the absolute bottom. And if you sell now you'll know you've sold the absolute bottom, which obviously is a very bad financial decision. So as a baggie in this timeline, you are cooked : you can't sell, you can only buy more.

62424645
>>62424635
I'm asking what the bull case is (if there even still is one), not price speculation. I'm not buying anymore and would only sell if there's another market pump.

62424654
>>62424645
> I'm asking what the bull case is (if there even still is one)
>has a 10k stack
Kys. All fields

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62424724
>>62424625
the funny thing is you realized this too late., '

luckilyi sold a shit load of it at 24 last year,

the writing was on the wall//

the onlything that got me was it didnt get close to breaking all time high... i thought it would at least hit 80 this bull with bitcoin...


we were all wrong, and no one and nobody made it from link..

its time for all to admit 10k was indeed a suicide stack.... 100k was the make it stack

62424738
>>62424667
it will probably hit 12-15 dollars in a couple more years in the next bull cycle...

unload it then...


link was too little too late. it was a cool project, its just that even after all these years it is becoming more clear that there is really no way for the token to capture meaningful value.

imagine if there was a way we could have all donated to https , that would have not even made a differnce

62424752
would you like $70,000 now?
or $8,000 in 5 years?
those are your options

62424809
>>62424625
Sell at $35 next cycle top

62424832
If Chainlink fails so does everything else.ive been holding since 2019. Even if I sold it for bitcoin the best I’d do is maybe double my money.
I’m not even remotely interested in any other crypto.

62424878
>>62424832
what does "everything else" failing even look like, stinky. the truth is, you're obsessed over a game that nobody else is playing. things are just gonna chug along as is, forever.

62424911
>>62424878
Without Link everything becomes ‘trust me’ and we all know how that ends. No utility just speculation and gambling

62424916
>>62424724
>too late., '
>>62424738
>to https , that

Literal jeet typing
Hope the rupees they pay you to fud are worth your soul

62424949
>2037
>staking v.3 finally released after years of delays
>cap still 15k
>mfw

62424983
>>62424625
Yes. You can hold it for the tech, oracles should be a public good so im happy to support sergey, its not about the money for me. Im helping the network

62425011
I’m holding cuz society sucks anyway

62425040
i have 60k link... if this doesn't get me rich i can't transition and be a league streamer..

62425115
>>62424625
No reason to hold it any more. I think the Clarity Act and the Payment Abstraction Layer coming online are the last chances to pump. Regardless what the price will be, I'll be selling in 2028/2029.

I just checked the chart several days ago and realized LINK had a local top at $26 on August 22, 2025. Four months later Sandisk started going parabolic. Fuck, if I sold off and rotated into stocks I could have retired by now.

62425139
Just a wait and see

62425162
>>62425139
wait and see you lose even more money?
wait and see you grow out as life passes you by?
wait and see how everyone in your peer group achieves all their goals and dreams while you languish in poverty forever?

62425170
I'm so tired.

62425228
hey do you guys use uniswap, cowswap or metamask swap for swapping shit on eth? does metamask just use uniswap?

62425248
>>62425115
>Payment Abstraction Layer
Agreed, but it's tied to Staking.
Yeah, those things represent last pump "events" but after everything's online either we see a steady climb up (as institutional adoption climbs) or price stagnates. If it stagnates YoY then yeah it's time to bail.

62425271
>>62424654
10k stack is small as shit, chainlink was $0.25-0.50 for a while.

t.10k+ holder, also demoralized and have no idea what bullcase for link is anymore besides the staking narrative but that seems to not ever be happening

62425287
>>62425271
doesn't matter what it used to be, dumb stinkie
right now it's worth $70,000
that's a big chunk of change
one you would realize right now if you weren't so fucking stupid

62425298
>>62425271

the bull case was that there would have been more hype behind the token...

as i have been fudding for years, it was all priced in all of it.

the funny thing is we were all right , meme magic was real , it all came true, even sergey at the fcuking white house with trump...

i literally made memes of sergey and trump in 2019...

we did meme it. to reality the only one that didnt come true was 1000 eoy


it didnt come true yet which is why i wont unload my 10k suicide stack....

t sold about 40k link since 2021 when link was 40 dollars.

62425469
>>62425298
>t sold about 40k link since 2021 when link was 40 dollars.
The only stinklinker who made

62425493
>>62424685
Yeh but meme dates keep me chugging along
>>62424713
You somehow lost 20k on LINK and call me low iq???

62425511
it's just money, just don't sell for a couple more years, ok? just hold, please

62425701
>>62424625
No:
>>62417503

62425716
>>62425493
>Somehow
Linkie average on this board are down 70% literally. Link since Trump revived crypto is underperforming Terra Luna's death spiraled fucking LUNC. Lol NO ONE is up on link and all of early biz sold in the first pump when it was clear they were not building out for token holders and made zero attempt at real utility for the token itself while they sold from operator rewards that were supposed to be ringfenced. Even now link is overpriced, unable to support even 30% of the network operations from revenues with no prospect or effort at changing that. It was always empty hype on headlines that mean nothing for the token that chainlink labs sells on the basis of.

62425736
>>62424667
it's your own fault you didn't sell, you had it in your hands. Nobody but your greed forced you to hold. Be honest anon, you were just greedy, nothing wrong with admitting that.

62425777
the real hopium now is that dtcc is going live this year
maybe the market reacts to that, maybe not
also that swift might be as well
i have to admit i thought it would pump on swift being confirmed but no, nothing
so will it pump with swift goes live? maybe not
will it pump with transactions? maybe not
it might take fee capture on a decent volume transactions to see anything and that could be 5 years away, easily
a long, long way from 2017 era when stuff would pump on rumors
the worst thing is that link is the best project in market, it should be half eth's market cap on speculations which would have it over $100 but it just doesn't move

62425798
>>62424666
>>62424911
>>62425011
>>62425511
>>62425777
holy fuck praise kek checked thread

62426804
>>62425287
I don't know about this anon, but I'm 38. $70k doesn't move the needle of my finances in any meaningful way. Maybe if I was 25 and wanted to live like a nigger for the summer.

item.title: WARNING: RIPPLE XRP ***EXTREME*** SUPER EMERGENCY!!! — 22 replies — 79.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
WARNING: RIPPLE XRP ***EXTREME*** SUPER EMERGENCY!!!

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-29 15:03 UTC

OP

Insiders at Ripple officially speculate the XRP going to moon very high soon with the clarity act after the 4th of July, according to rumors, analysts say. This is huge and **MASSIVE** news for XRP and the cryptocurrency community as a whole.

You. Are. Not. Bullish. Enough.

Early replies (first 3)

62420069
>>62420066
shut the fuck up

62420100
>>62420066
XRP is a slush fund for banks. That is why they partnered with it.

62420110
So bullish

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62420119
Yes

62420157
Alright why is it pumping so hard?

62420174
>>62420157
It’s only up about 2% at the moment. Even 5% up isn’t much.

62420277
>>62420174
Bullish

62420439
BOOOOOOOOOOOM

62420471
>>62420066
XRP died in 2024

62420479
>>62420066
I sold this scam the last pump attempt.

Thanks retards

62420480
>>62420471
>ATH was after 2024
>It died before that

Based retard

62420531
>>62420066
HOLY FXCKING BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62420698
>>62420110
Fkn bullish

62420798
Please stay in your containment thread. Thank you for your consideration.

62421769
>>62420066
So bullish xrp

62423981
>>62420066
KAAAABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62424278
>>62420066
its boomish...

62425201
So bullish

62425220
>>62420066
These threads are months past being stale memeing.

62425222
HOLY SHIT
XRP WILL ZA WARUDO TO THE MOON

62426624
holy shit supply might be low on all exchange wallets, what the fuck is going on

62426848
>>62425220
Bullish

item.title: WE are the oil marines and we're not fucking selling — 20 replies — 55.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
WE are the oil marines and we're not fucking selling

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-29 14:58 UTC

OP

Another cargo ship just got blown up in the strait. They can't keep oil down forever.

Early replies (first 3)

62411834
>it’s true

62412180
I slurped some more oil

62413818
>we are the oil baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413900
you can sell now at 70
or at 40 when the Ukraine Russia war ends
your choice

62416450
>>62413900
Only a matter of time before reality hits the market. The emergency reserves are not infinite and it will take years to repair the damages in the gulf and in Russia.

62416754
>>62416450
They are actually 1 week away from losing pressure. Iran knows this and is in no rush.

62416764
>>62416450
This is doomer bullshit.

62417845
>>62416764
Look up direct quotes from gulf states about the amount of damage and how long it will take to go back to normal production. Even without the destruction, just the fact that they had to halt production means it will take months to reactivate facilities.
But who am I kidding, you won't look up shit because you are a stubborn low iq peak dunning kruger ass who won't change his mind anyway.

62417869
Oil reserves are the lowest that they have been in ~40 years. Even Sleepy Joe was chewing through reserves to suppress prices and that was without the turmoil in Iran.

The reserves have to be replenished. Even if the conflict ends tomorrow without any more turbulence, the damage has already been done. Some people buy a small position in oil as a hedge for their tech investments. If anything, I have a small position in tech as a hedge and most of my money in oil. Everyone will be crying come September and I will be sitting on fat stacks of cash courtesy of black gold while reminding them that I warned them.

62417932
Sell those bags when you get the chance, strikes happening now but idk how much more people will care

62417947
>>62416450
>it will take years to repair the damages in Russia.
So far, it has taken Russia less than a week to repair crude export terminals. Crude oil export volumes have not been affected.

Refinery damage is harder to see, however it's not nearly as much as the damage in the Gulf, because 50kg warheads simply do not cause much damage

62417949
>>62411825
The price is the price. It reflects a consensus amongst oil traders who are very sophisticated. Why do you think you possess knowledge that they don't? I think some oil stocks can be a good investment but derivatives on the commodities or ETFs that invest in them are a gamble.

62418308
>>62411825
The war hasnt ended and the "ceasefire" is fake and gay

expect $160 a barrel

62418377
Sure, you have the facts on your side. The bears have every world leader on their side, every industrial titan, trillions of dollars of economic output that all depends on oil staying affordable. You're a fool to challenge their ability to continue denying reality, they'll do whatever it takes to keep the numbers healthy.

62420386
>>62418377
That is literally what are those people already doing to keep the price under $100. After a certain point though, they won't be able to hold on anymore.

62421774
>>62413818
>oil barrelies

62421795
>>62416450
>he thinks the real reserves are openly communicated and updated

62423372
>>62421795
Educate me about those super secret subterranean 10 dimensional reserves

62423378
>>62411825
Everytime we have these outsized moves on some random FOTM bullshit e.g GME/BBBY/LUNC/Oil a bunch of you retards develop an emotional attachment to it instead of just trading it and leaving when the attention moves on

62426824
>>62423378
$150 EOY

item.title: In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f… — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-29 15:15 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62425500
if you can get laid in the west you wont attract good women in the 3rd world, you are just going to pay the access to brown 5/10's

62425521
>>62425500
This is saarcope and you know it. Tiny browlettes in jungle countries unironically melt when 6ft snowchads pinch their tits.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62425535
>>62425420
If you're asian, unattractive, with a small dick, and money, then obviously your sexual strategy will be to be a passport bro and downplay the importance of looks.

62425559
Get the toonder in passport mode, plenty of post wall turd world might consider making a romantic life with him

62425994
>>62425420
love doesn't exist. once we accept this, it's easy to understand that being scammed by women in third world countries is much cheaper than being scammed by women in first world countries.

62426288
>>62425521
kek
fr I like getting my shaft creamed on by non white chicks

62426850
>>62425420
why is the dude always so negative, is it because he is low test? he seems like a lil bitch ngl

62426867
>>62426288

62426887

>loses all his money leverage gambling

62426905

62426910

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 17 replies — 33.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-29 14:49 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62426537
It will probably stabilize around $20k, dip down to $10k, then stabilize again around $500 or so. From there it will hover between $480 and $520 for a few decades before slowly petering out.

62426546
>>62426535
Time to long now?

62426550
>>62426512
Turned it into USDC and waiting. Don't wanna give up on it entirely and fiat out 'cause governmentberg will tax me.

62426552
Sometimes I wonder if some anons are mentally retarded or what...

We've been talking about the $20k-$30k bottom in October 2026 for over 6 months now, and every day someone like OP asks if they should buy now or wait...

62426585
>>62426552
THIS

just WAIT

62426587
>>62426537
Thanks for the financial advice. I'll set my buy order at $499 just to be safe.

62426626
>>62426550
How much tasks percentage?

62426636
>>62426585
Wait until?

62426638
Waiting for king of teh retards to dump. Doesnt matter what the price is. When he is done and all the blood is flushed from the street then i am back in the game

62426647
>>62426626
19

62426649
>>62426638
He is rich

62426733
>>62426512
you dumb inhuman fuck do you think anyone will invest in your meme coin when there are real assets that are going up 100%+ every month on the stock market?

62426759
>>62426649

62426775
>>62426537

item.title: Working as a mailman? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Working as a mailman?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-29 15:06 UTC

OP

Anyone have experience with working for the post office?
I just got a job offer as letter carrier (CCA), so I thought it might be worth a shot since I'm a skill-less loser with zero prospects.
Starting pay is $21/hr but I guess you can make $30/hr in a few years.
Only downside is a 30 minute commute but it's an affluent white neighborhood.

Early replies (first 3)

62424345
Surrogacy is literally just renting and it is cheaper than buying. Why haven’t you go surrogate maxxing. You aren’t trying to own something so you can be happy? Cook

62424404
>>62424298
It's fucking awesome. You have to eat shit for 3 to 6 months based on your learning curve, I've seen the rare case were someone picks it up immediately but roughly 3 to 6 months until you really figure it out. Stick it out because once you figure it out you've got arguably the comfiest job imaginable.

62424472
>>62424404
Interesting. Please tell me what makes it comfy, anon. Is it the idea of being by yourself and walking? Like freedom?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62424874
>>62424298
I was really close to doing it. Got an orientation date but chickened out. From what I hear it’s an ok deal for someone who wants to stack cash and doesn’t mind working a ton of hours. Top pay is in the high 30s per hour but you only get it after 12 years. Overtime after 8 hours and double time after 10 or 12 hours I cant remember. During the holiday season there’s opportunities for triple time. The starting pay seems kinda low but it’s pretty likely that you’ll get 55+ hours per week. Do you watch mailman nick on youtube? He started with usps 3 years ago and made 90k+ last year.

I think ups drivers make more but also are worked a lot harder.

62424980
>>62424874
Tell me more about the differences between usps, fedex, amazon, and ups please

62426363
>>62424980
Amazon is the worst. They’re all crappy contractor companies that only work with amazon. The average hourly wage has gotten a little better but it’s not enough.

Fedex ground is trash. You’e also working for a contractor. Pay is better than amazon but there is little room for growth. They also deliver heavy shit like tires and dog food.
Fedex express is ok. You’re directly employed by fedex and top pay is low 30s per hour. That’s a livable wage if you ask me.

Usps is probably 2nd best. Possible pension if you last that long (lol). Probably never gonna be defunct. Top pay is 40 per hour after 12 years. Letters are lighter than packages so less damaging to your body.

Ups is the best deal. Top pay is mid 40s per hour after 4 years. Also have a pension. It’s hard to become a driver right away though. They want you to pay your dues as a warehouse worker first. Sometimes it takes years for you to become a package car driver. They also do furloughs which won’t work for everyone. Definitely highest earning potential. Sounds like the definition of golden handcuffs to me. You can make 145K and have a pension but running in and out of a delivery truck 200 times a day for a couple decades… they definitely earn it. You can transfer to other departments with enough seniority. They train their own truck drivers and yard switchers which is much better on your body.

62426862
>>62424298
I was a cca for almost a year. You can be a cca for up to two year before youre mandatory converted into a career position. You need to be on top of your shit for the first 90 days. Dont call out, do things by the book. After that it wasnt too bad. I enjoyed being on my own, walking for miles and listening to podcasts all day. The trucks dont have ac or heat but that might have changed with the new ones. Supervisors are always retarded assholes, so make friends with the shop steward and know the rules. Youll probably get a lot of overtime, including all holidays, which I didnt like, but many others wanted. Its good for someone without skills and connections

item.title: >boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >PIP requiernments is classical non sensical bullshit >dont even come close to fuflin… — 51 replies — 27.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >PIP requiernments is classical non sensical bullshit >dont even come close to fuflin…

Open thread • Replies: 51 • Updated: 2026-06-29 13:28 UTC

OP

>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago
>PIP requiernments is classical non sensical bullshit
>dont even come close to fuflining any of it anyways
>PIP ended last Tuesday
>haven't heard anything from anyone, still employed
Am I getting fired or not? I got a feeling im not out of the woods and they are just collecting paperwork. My boss said he had to talk to HR when i asked him about my PIP 2 weeks ago. The omly chace i have is that they were gonna fire me but deicded to keep me for a bit longer for some tax reason or something. I job hopped a lot and needed to last at this company for a few years to restore my reputation.. Really paranoid about losing it cuz im fucked if i do. Genuinely thinking of becoming a fetenoyl dealer beacuse being a career cuck is the worst torture imaginable

Early replies (first 3)

62423284
Most companies are downsizing so yeah, if they put you on PIP there's like a 99% chance they're just doing the legal mumbo jumbo so you can't sue after they fire you

62423286
>>62423284
Why haven't they fired me yet? Its fucked up that they are downsizing yet trying to make us feel like we arent worth dog shit and are chasing giant problems for the comapny by existing when we are really actually working hard the whole time

62423287
>>62423286
>Why haven't they fired me yet?
Like I said, companies need to go through a lot of legal mumbo jumbo so the fired employee can't sue them for wrongful termination

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423516
>>62423508
oh ur chilling if u get interviews then dude eventually u will get an offer.

62423531
>>62423516
Nope, its been like this for 4 months now. Never had no many interviews and so little luck. Its not luck or my skills. These are ghost jobs, they are never planning on hiring anyone. Look it up..

62423536
>>62423434
>Don't people usually get fired after a PIP
ftfy. And yes. But there's not really a time limit where they have to fire you within x days of the end of your PIP. The purpose of a PIP is to establish that you are unable to achieve acceptable performance levels, they can fire you at any time after that. It sounds like they're probably still interviewing your replacement.

62423543
>>62423531
Nobody goes through the expense of conducting interviews for ghost jobs though. You're just not good enough.

62423546
>>62423543
>Nobody goes through the expense of conducting interviews
you aren't familiar with HR roasties who need to justify their salary are you

62423552
>>62423546
For real. These women have nothing to do all day long and they love sitting in meetings because it makes them feel important.

62423557
>>62423543
>>62423546
He also isn't familiar with the fact that the HR roasites are actually helping their company by enabling keniving behavior like doing fake interviews so investory falsely think they are growing, excuses to hire overbroad or promote internallyfor 1/4th the prices cuz they legally have to try to hire someone in the US first or just general data gathering that they sell off to supplement their income. The HR roasitie probably pays for her salary and then some alone just form selling all the data..

62423561
>>62423543
Also, after this many interviews, its statistically impossible that its my fault at this point..

62423573
>>62423561
You are the common denominator in your repeated failures to get hired.

62423575
dude
they literally have scent receptors for Cluster A
it's your fault the way you were born

62423587
>>62423573
>muh common denominator
Ironically its the same problem who say
>causation doemst equal correlation

Unwilling to look deeper into subject and never took an actual probability class. Ghost jobs are everywere right now.
>>62423575
Im in engineering so idk why it would be a problem for them anyways.

62423819
>>62423278
The PIP shit is just so they have an excuse not to give you a raise.

62424578
>>62423819
They already denied me my raise before putting me on the PIP. If its just that I will be relived

62424616
>>62423286
Really hope you started looking for another job, the PIP shit is just to cover for legal issues, so they can fire you without compensation depending on which fucked up state you live in. Don't panic but do look for another job. A PIP has never ever saved anyone unless you actually pass it so they have to wait a few months before trying again

62424656
>>62423291
They probably forgot anon. Has happened to me many times, I even blow off entire jobs sometimes on the hope that everyone just forgets.

62424748
>>62423286
>really actually working hard the whole time
There's your problem

62425104
>>62423278
Take the hint anon, they already put you on a pip so they want to get rid of you.

No employer puts a member of staff on a pip that they want to keep.

62425117
>>62423531
wonder what the purpose of doing this is

62425123
>>62423291
>PIP is over
Lmao. PIP is never over. They're throwing your wagie ass a life preserver of being able to find a job while you're still employed. It's a win-win for both of you right now. You can job hunt and they don't have to send your shit to legal to start justifying firing you. Ask your manager if you completed your PIP to satisfaction, if they say yes work on your resume. If they say no you have like 2 months before you have to explain why you lost your last job while applying to 20 jobs a day.

62425193
Once you get a pip, you can just stop caring entirely and stop doing your job until they fire you, right? There's no advantage to playing it out because you're not going to qualify for unemployment anyways?

62425239
And now you're always a target. You'll always have that mark. That's only if you make it though. Typically you're not supposed to anyway. PIP is just a means of managing someone out.
Don't think so?
>anon made some mistakes with his work and could do better
>person 1 gets off the record advice and coaching to improve
>person 2 gets documented instances of feedback (paper trail)
>person 1 just does the improvement and keeps going along and it's like nothing ever happened
>person 2 now has company processes and paperwork showing me he has room for improvement and here's the action plan you can do to be considered worthy of your job, maybe does it and passes, always has that mark, affects potential promotions, raises, etc and typically just is gotten rid of anyway
If you're on PIP, you're not wanted. Simple as.
If they wanted you to simply improve they would simply get on call with you and tell you what you can do better without any of the docs. Docs are just suws to get rid of you while having a legit appearance so they don't risk getting sued.

62425640
>>62425239
>>62423329
read my post loser, its just toxic management

in my case i had a brown middle manager that could not stand the fact that i was not answering his teams messages in less than 2 hours and also that i never asked for my payslips when all my coworkers were crying like bitches everytime the salary was late for 1-2 days

when we got laid off we had a 2 months notice (its the rule in my country) and during two months i made fun of him constantly, i was even travelling during my shift and blocked his number when he called me as i was in the taxi in bangkok

62426158
>>62423278
Why did they put you on a PIP? Can you give a good faith recounting of their reasons and not just
>fucking bullshit
>my manager is a cunt
>toxic workplace blah blah blah

62426163
PIP is currently closed due to AIDS. You forgot to use a condom on Juneteenth.

62426164
Once you are doubted at work it's the beginning of the end.
Start looking elsewhere.

62426165
>>62425193
Correct.

62426309
>>62423397
op keeps making this same post weekly.

1. he is poor. this is why he is so scared of getting fired.
2. he isn't that bright (see #1).

how can you be posting on /biz/ and not have a giant stack in at least something. seriously, most anons here at least have 25k in soxl, gold, or fucking pokemon cards. do better anon.

62426321
>>62426309
25K?

kek

you can't even buy a garage in bulgaria with this

62426369
>>62426321
for an emergency fund you illiterate negro. 25k in theory would be enough to float him enough to find new employment so that he isn't forced to continue letting his manager control his life.

62426383
>>62426369
he can just quiet quit instead of spending his savings

>>62425017

62425033

62425063

62425081

Normies are always 1 step away from persecution. It's why they still fear the holohoax because they instinctually know they can be declared persona non grata at any time so they make themselves small and blend in.

62425090
>>62425017
Crack didn't kill them fast enough, so now they're hooking them on sugar so they kill themselves slowly but legally

62425312
>>62425017

62425956
>>62425090
Give them diabetus then give them obamacare

62426114
>>62425017
ebt is quantitative easing for consumer staples

62426126
>>62425312

62426133
>>62426126
Are you crying because he is fucking all the women you go exploring with? Don't be a simp. Lol and wonder why she like when he is up her ass. Yeah he is fucking her. Moron. He makes then you every month so think about that. Lol

item.title: Thinking seriously of finally capitulating and selling my 12,000 DOT at ATL of $0.80 and realizing a 100k net loss Bitco… — 22 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Thinking seriously of finally capitulating and selling my 12,000 DOT at ATL of $0.80 and realizing a 100k net loss Bitco…

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-29 13:30 UTC

OP

Thinking seriously of finally capitulating and selling my 12,000 DOT at ATL of $0.80 and realizing a 100k net loss

Bitcoin is more likely to go to 500k than dot going to $8

It's over

What do you think

Early replies (first 3)

62423104
Why on earth did you invest in that garbage in the first place?

62423107
>>62423104
My friend convinced me. Wish he was never born. Cocksucker

62423116
>>62423107
it must be traumatizing, I have seen you in like 4 different threads. And I actually do believe you, no LARP goes for this long. There will be more opportunities, maybe it's not in equities or speculative "assets" this time.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423121
>>62423107
honestly dude this sounds insane but in the sheer absolute bumfuck rarity that DOT recovers. you're going to commit suicide if it moons. I know this sounds highly irrational but $9600 is inconsequential money unless you have debt. it sounds fucking nuts but maybe just park it safely and just genuinely forget about it.

62423122
>>62423121
>you're going to commit suicide if it moons
if you realize losses now*

this is purely psychological, it's shit financial advice and it's a gamblers fallacy but im telling you the way you're talking about this shows me if DOT moons you will not cope. It's already traumatizing missing out on the semiconductor/AI boom then having to miss the very asset you invested in recover would make ME want to blow my brains out and I have never had suicidal depression

62423140
>>62423116
>>62423121
>>62423122
Least insane DOT baggie

62423143
>>62423140
dumb misunderstanding but I get it, I am just saying 88% loss is devastating. if it was options, people just let it expire at that point

62423206
>>62423121
9600 dollar is still a huge amount to let go to zero

62423207
>>62423066
Polkadot will have a glorious comeback.

62423212
>>62423207
This confirms it's going to zero, the troll bot arrived

62423218
>>62423116
My life is ruined. Even though by the grace of God I managed to earn a good living and financially keep body and soul together, it haunts me every day. And dot is less than half of my total wreckage. We are talking 250k down the hole net loss

62423224
>>62423212
You know what's funny? I'm not even the guy who usually posts these. I just downloaded this image and started pretending to be him. I've been doing it for a few weeks now, and I've never bought a cent of DOT.

62423227
>>62423224
>shill decay to the point people are doing ironic shills
grim

62423253
>>62423227
The current 'Wendy's General' is also mine. I thought people would just make fun of it, but most took it seriously. I think that there are other ironic shills, the 'Polkadot General' wasn't mine.

62423261
>>62423212
Bear market. Remember this

62423309
>>62423261
We aren't in a bear market yet

62423332
>>62423066
Sell, treat yourself to a trip with the proceeds to unwind, and then make a fresh start with genuine assets.

62424184
>>62423066
>He didn't set a stop loss
Dumbfuck

62424714
>>62423104
Back then I recall DOT was getting a lot of support by the sorts of people that want to enslave everybody and to some cynical fucks investing in something that the people that salivate about fake crypto govie tokens that auto-track and auto-tax seemed like a safe bet.

62424732
>>62423066
Well I can tell you at very least it wont recover in the next 31 days so you can at least loss harvest for your taxes, and buy it back then. Probably at a much lower price.

Or it moons to 1k and you want to shoot me. So yea not investment advice, just saying you could if you wanted to.

62425540
>>62423066
>this is purely psychological, it's shit financial advice and it's a gamblers fallacy but im telling you the way you're talking about this shows me if DOT

Dude just DCA

Its 80 fucking cents

You can get your position cost average down 70% if you throw like 16k in it now

62426389
>>62423066
Pretty sure we've reached rock bottom. I'm still holding my crypto project, no matter how little I believe DOT will pump I think we're set for an whale accumulation phase. There are more solid project out there, though. Also new more favorably shilled upcoming ones that can gain the hype some of us chase, but I hold on my original thesis on my project. The WEF'ers are in on Sol as the new Eth, however buck broken the code is they're only interested in control. There are a lot of new liquidity focused copies of this appearing the top 50 exactly now.
A good indicator would be how much seasonal slander is going on. There's no slander on Gavin for being gay or his staff from jumping ship to the project DOT copied their consensus mechanism from. You will have to learn how to find trustworthiness in people who deliver on their promises. I wouldn't have dreamed of how much Monero gained confidence, lately. I honestly had thought the decoupling from major exchanges was dooming them ,but they're ahead of the curve.

item.title: hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gai… — 12 replies — 4.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gai…

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-29 12:44 UTC

OP

hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gains. and what a delicious treat to see when i returned: link struggling to stay above $7 and fat serg canceling the build program. LMAO. you stinkoids just can't catch a break huh. honestly how do ya'll have any hope left at this point. what is going to cause link to break $10, let alone $50? kekkerinos.

Early replies (first 3)

62424357
>>62424306
We've missed you. :(

62424375
After a big pump and dump, assets seldom pump again. It happened once with BTC but it's rare. Link is unlikely to see serious gains again.

62424382
>>62424375
The big banks will pump our bags once they start using LINK in earnest by 2030 to run the entire financial infrastructure.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62424407
>>62424306

62424429

62424443
Glad to hear you had a nice time, kekkarino!

62425371

62425397
>>62424306

>>62424375
>>62424382
>>62424407

Fellow link holders we should just ignore this guy.

He is spreading fud about our investment and he is wrong

Can't the mods just ban him?

I mean I know this is 4chan and all and we like to joke and be mean to one another but as a multiple year long chainlink holder I have my limits

Anyway i'm back to my job cleaning toilets now

62425406
>>62424382
i find great satisfction that there are people like you who never took any profit at all...

i get great pleasure knowing it just gets worse and worse for you as time goes on,


i get great pleasure knowing your lasts breaths will be that you sadly had to pass away before link pumped

62425455
>>62424306

62425517

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-29 12:03 UTC

OP

Knew about it at fucking $100k market cap but dismissed it as another stupid scam rug. Low and behold it goes 1000x. Could've become a millionaire and escaped this stupid space forever. Just fuck everything and most especially fuck the stupid X+SOL ecosystem.

62422604
brutal

62422608
>>62422583

62422619

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62422626
>>62422619
Damn. Even the scam rugpullers are struggling to find liquidity.

62422679
>>62422583
1.2% liquidity wow. And insider supply? Man /biz/ we need to pump Foodpepe to 10m to finally win again againt the cabal.

62422847
This is what I hate about this faggot ass board.
I get it the gay captchas are necessary to stop bots and jeets but shitcoin threads shouldn't be deleted.
Everyone is an adult here and the decision to buy or not something it's up to each individual.
BRING BACK SHITCOIN GENERALS

62423214
>>62422847

62423260
>>62422847

No faggot.

62423264
>>62422583

You forget the hundreds of rugs you avoided, while hyperfocusing on the few things you missed out on. You're not a psychic so stop trying to think like one

62423347
>>62423214
It's scamlana
every shitter is a low liquidity asset
we can just go back to shill ETH plays like the old times, you know?

62423685
>>62423347
Shitlana needs to unironically die. The fucking billion rugpulls made by useless retards every single fucking hour killed the alt season.
No one is taking crypto seriously when shitlana exists.

62423716
>>62423685
>>62423347
That means they're doing something right, don't act as if other chains are holier than thou they're all useless and serve nothing but to give an avenue for gamblers to be complete degenerates.

62423830
>>62422583

Imagine having this kind of money and still trying to scam a bunch of poorfags out of $50 lol

62426149
>>62423830
Analos....
